apiroute.title: URI of the WIFI controller.
apiroute.description: Should be in the format of: http://aaa.bbb.ccc.ddd or https://aaa.bbb.ccc.ddd

temperatureOffsetOutside.title: Temperature-Offset-Outside
temperatureOffsetOutside.description: Allows an offset to calibrate the measurement.

temperatureOffsetInside.title: Temperature-Offset-Inside
temperatureOffsetInside.description: Allows an offset to calibrate the measurement.

uuid.title: Client UUID token if needed
uuid.description: Should be a 32 digit hex string. Most Daikin devices do not need a UUID token. Read the documentation: https://github.com/cbrandlehner/homebridge-daikin-local#httpsregistered-client-support

system.title: Controller Type
system.description: Select your WiFi controller type: 'Default' for standard Daikin WiFi modules (BRP069xxx), 'Skyfi' for Skyfi controllers, or 'Faikout' for ESP32-Faikout open-source controllers. Choose 'Faikout' if you installed the ESP32-Faikout firmware on an ESP32 chip.

swingMode.title: Swing Direction Preference
swingMode.description: Swing direction used when the Oscillate toggle is ON in HomeKit (1 = vertical, 2 = horizontal, 3 = 3D). This does not turn swing on or off — use the HomeKit Oscillate control for that. All controller types support this setting.

disableFan.title: Disable Separate Fan Accessory
disableFan.description: When enabled (true), hides the separate Fan accessory from the Home app main view. The plugin still exposes fan speed and oscillation on the HeaterCooler service, but the Home app may not show these controls on all iOS versions. If Oscillate is hard to find, leave this disabled and show the AC and Fan as separate tiles in HomeKit.

fanMode.title: Fan mode
fanMode.description: If in DRY-mode, switching on the FAN in Apple-Home will start the AC in dry mode. If in FAN-mode, it will only activate the FAN.

fanPowerMode.title: Fan power mode
fanPowerMode.description: Should Fan power only affect the fan or complete device.

fanName.title: FAN name
fanName.description: The name of the fan. Only applies if the FAN has not been disabled.

defaultMode.title: Default operation mode
defaultMode.description: When turning on the Daikin device in HomeBridge using a single-tap, the default operation mode will be applied. The Daikin-Auto-mode does not match Apple-Homekit - so it is advised to use either COOL- or HEAT-mode if possible.

enableHumiditySensor.title: Humidity sensor enabled
enableHumiditySensor.description: Enables humidity sensor if present in Daikin device.

enableEconoMode.title: Econo Mode switch enabled
enableEconoMode.description: Enables a switch in HomeKit to toggle the AC's Econo mode (energy-saving mode). Works with both traditional Daikin and Faikout controllers. Mutually exclusive with Powerful mode.

econoModeName.title: Econo Mode switch name (optional)
econoModeName.description: Custom name for the Econo Mode switch. Default: 'Econo Mode'. Toggle the switch and check Homebridge logs to identify which one it is.

enablePowerfulMode.title: Powerful Mode switch enabled
enablePowerfulMode.description: Enables a switch in HomeKit to toggle the AC's Powerful mode (maximum cooling/heating). Works with both traditional Daikin and Faikout controllers. Mutually exclusive with Econo mode.

powerfulModeName.title: Powerful Mode switch name (optional)
powerfulModeName.description: Custom name for the Powerful Mode switch. Default: 'Powerful Mode'. Toggle the switch and check Homebridge logs to identify which one it is.

enableNightQuietMode.title: Night Quiet Mode switch enabled
enableNightQuietMode.description: Enables a switch in HomeKit to toggle the AC's Night Quiet mode (silent operation). Faikout uses fan='Q', traditional Daikin uses f_rate='B' (silent). Can be used with Econo or Powerful modes.

nightQuietModeName.title: Night Quiet Mode switch name (optional)
nightQuietModeName.description: Custom name for the Night Quiet switch. Default: 'Night Quiet'. Toggle the switch and check Homebridge logs to identify which one it is.

enableFanSpeedInSettings.title: Enable Fan Speed in AC Settings
enableFanSpeedInSettings.description: When enabled (default), adds a fan speed slider directly in the AC accessory settings for easy access. Disable this if you only want to use the separate Fan accessory for fan speed control.

enableOscillationInSettings.title: Enable Oscillation in AC Settings
enableOscillationInSettings.description: When enabled (default), exposes oscillation on the HeaterCooler service for the Home app AC settings panel. Visibility depends on HomeKit layout (combined vs separate tiles). The separate Fan accessory always carries oscillate when disableFan is false.

enableVerticalSwingSwitch.title: Enable Vertical Swing Switch (Faikout only)
enableVerticalSwingSwitch.description: Adds a separate switch in HomeKit to independently control vertical (up/down) swing. Combined with the Horizontal Swing switch, this gives you 3 modes: vertical only, horizontal only, or 3D (both). Only works with Faikout controllers.

verticalSwingName.title: Vertical Swing switch name (optional)
verticalSwingName.description: Custom name for the Vertical Swing switch. Default: 'Vertical Swing'.

enableHorizontalSwingSwitch.title: Enable Horizontal Swing Switch (Faikout only)
enableHorizontalSwingSwitch.description: Adds a separate switch in HomeKit to independently control horizontal (left/right) swing. Combined with the Vertical Swing switch, this gives you 3 modes: vertical only, horizontal only, or 3D (both). Only works with Faikout controllers.

horizontalSwingName.title: Horizontal Swing switch name (optional)
horizontalSwingName.description: Custom name for the Horizontal Swing switch. Default: 'Horizontal Swing'.

quietWebSocketLogging.title: Quiet WebSocket Logging (Faikout only)
quietWebSocketLogging.description: When enabled (default), reduces log noise from WebSocket heartbeat and button status updates. Status messages will use debug level instead of info level. Set to false for verbose debugging.

minTemperature.title: Minimum Temperature
minTemperature.description: Minimum temperature setting for your AC unit in Celsius. Most Daikin units support 10°C for heating and 18°C for cooling.

maxTemperature.title: Maximum Temperature
maxTemperature.description: Maximum temperature setting for your AC unit in Celsius. Most Daikin units support 30-32°C.

response.title: Response
response.description: Timeout in milliseconds for the device to start responding.  Default: 2000 (2 seconds).

deadline.title: Deadline
deadline.description: Timeout in milliseconds for the device to complete a response.  Default: 60000 (one minute).

retries.title: Retries
retries.description: The plugin can retry to reconnect in case of a connection problem. Default: 5.

layout.helpvalue:
🔌 Controller Type Selection
  • Default: Standard Daikin WiFi modules (BRP069xxx series)
  • Skyfi: Skyfi WiFi controller models
  • Faikout: ESP32-Faikout open-source controller (uses JSON API instead of query strings) - Visit ESP32-Faikout GitHub → for installation guides and compatible hardware lists.

Faikout controllers automatically use separate horizontal/vertical swing controls and JSON-based API endpoints.

items.helpvalue:
💡 Fan Control Options:

1. In AC Settings: Adds fan speed and oscillation to the HeaterCooler service (may appear in the AC settings panel depending on your iOS/HomeKit version)

2. Separate Fan Accessory (most reliable for Oscillate): Keep disableFan off, then in the Home app choose Show as Separate Tiles — use Oscillate from the Settings (gear) view on the AC or Fan tile

swingMode sets the direction when oscillate is ON (1 = vertical, 2 = horizontal, 3 = 3D), not on/off.

↕↔ Independent Swing Switches (Faikout only):

Enable separate switches for vertical and horizontal swing to get 3 swing modes:
• Vertical only - up/down airflow
• Horizontal only - left/right airflow
• 3D mode - both switches ON

These appear as separate toggles in the Home app and work independently of the main oscillation setting above.
Note: Econo and Powerful modes are mutually exclusive - enabling one will disable the other.

🌡️ Temperature Range Settings:
Configure the minimum and maximum temperature limits for your specific Daikin AC unit. Different models support different ranges.

🌐 Faikout WebSocket Settings:
For Faikout controllers (ESP32-Faikout), quiet logging reduces log noise from status updates while maintaining full functionality.

HTTPS / TLS:
No configuration needed. On Node.js 18+ (OpenSSL 3.x), the plugin automatically uses a legacy TLS agent for HTTPS controllers (required by many Daikin units, e.g. BRP072). Check Homebridge logs for TLS: HTTPS apiroute at startup.
